concerns.text: User Story 2 (Establish Measurement Precision for Core Invariants) lacks explicit FR linkage. US-2 requires precision validation for core invariants (crossing number, braid index) in Phase 1, but FR-003 only covers additional invariants (Phase 2+). No FR explicitly requires precision-threshold validation for core invariants in Phase 1, leaving the story without concrete implementation guidance.

FR-004 (exploratory data analysis with scatter plots of crossing number vs. braid index, stratified by alternating classification) is not referenced or validated by any Success Criterion. SC-001 through SC-014 do not include a criterion that explicitly validates FR-004's scatter plot requirement, creating a traceability gap.

KnotRecord entity definition in Key Entities section is inconsistent with the knot_record.schema.yaml contract. The spec defines KnotRecord with top-level source_timestamp and checksum_sha256 fields, but the contract expects these inside a nested source object. This schema mismatch will cause validation failures and represents a spec-internal consistency issue.

FR-001 downloads all prime knots ≤ 13 crossings, while FR-012 filters to hyperbolic knots (volume > 0) for analysis. This scope distinction is documented but could be clearer. Consider adding explicit cross-reference between FR-001 (download scope) and FR-012 (analysis scope) to prevent misinterpretation.

SC-001 states validation benchmark focuses on ≤ 10 crossings for Phase 1, while FR-001 downloads ≤ 13 crossings. This validation staging is documented in Assumptions, but SC-001 should explicitly reference the Assumptions section to clarify the distinction between data availability (≤ 13) and validated completeness (≤ 10).
